Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she uses [number] ounces of shampoo per week. A leap year has 366 days, so she washes her hair [number] times in a year. To calculate the total amount of shampoo she needs, we multiply the number of washes by 1/4 ounce. This gives us the total ounces required for the year. Since she buys shampoo in 14-ounce bottles, we divide the total ounces by 14 to determine how many bottles she needs. This calculation will help her plan her purchases accordingly.

If Brittany uses [number] ounces of shampoo in a leap year, dividing this by the size of each bottle, which is [number] ounces, will give the exact number of bottles needed. It’s important to round up to the nearest whole number since she cannot purchase a fraction of a bottle. This ensures she has enough shampoo to last the entire year without running out.
